topic: Absolute Value Function and its Graph

questions.question: The graph of $y = |x - 3|$ has a vertex at
The ancient Egyptian architect Imhotep designed a pyramid with a base length of $|x + 5|$ meters. If the length is 10 meters, what is $x$?
The graph of $y = |2x - 1|$ has an axis of symmetry at
The ancient Greek philosopher Aristotle measured the distance between two points as $|x - 2| + |x + 1|$. If the distance is 5, what is $x$?
The graph of $y = |x| + 2$ has a minimum value at
The ancient Chinese mathematician Liu Hui graphed the function $y = |x - 2| - 1$. What is the direction of opening?
The ancient Babylonian astronomer Kidinnu measured the angle of the sun as $|x - 30|$ degrees. If the angle is 10 degrees, what is $x$?
The graph of $y = -|x + 1|$ has a maximum value at
Graph the function $y = |x - 2| + 1$ and identify the vertex, axis of symmetry, and direction of opening. Show your work and explain your reasoning.
The ancient Mayan calendar measured time as $|x - 5| + |x + 2|$. If the time is 10, what is $x$? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to use graph paper to visualize the functions.
Tip 2: Remind students to check their work and explain their reasoning.
Tip 3: Suggest that students use different colors to highlight important features of the graphs.
